mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equities and tightens financing conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 4.49 for 2006-02-01, consistent with an ongoing tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not have lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with flat or falling FEDFUNDS in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, short-term policy becomes restrictive enough to compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 4.49 for 2006-02-01, immediately below the 4.5% threshold
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% show nonnegative 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in HOUST from an elevated level signals weakening residential investment and deteriorating credit-sensitive demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months after exceeding 2000
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 2276.0 for 2006-01-01, an elevated housing-starts level
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns greater than 10% from levels above 2000 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong RSAFS growth combined with rising FEDFUNDS indicates demand overheating that invites tighter policy, so the joint signal causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises more than 3% over 6 months and FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.50pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 362294.0 for 2006-02-01 while FEDFUNDS was 4.49
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term RSAFS growth times FEDFUNDS change has nonnegative predictive coefficient for 20-day S&P 500 returns in rolling walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS with UNRATE below 5% signals broad income growth without visible labor-market stress, supporting consumption and earnings and causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE remains below 5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 134789.0 and UNRATE was 4.8 for 2006-02-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-unemployment payroll-growth periods fail to produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than the unconditional sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals expanding real output and operating leverage for listed firms, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a 60-day forward return hit rate at or below the unconditional positive-return frequency in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is rising signals inflation pressure that forces further tightening, raising real-rate risk and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 1.0% over 3 months and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 199.0 for 2006-01-01 and FEDFUNDS was 4.49 for 2006-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL acceleration with rising FEDFUNDS is followed by average 20-day S&P 500 returns that are not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points from below 5% signals a transition from full employment toward recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months after being below 5%
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp from below 5% are not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than stable-UNRATE periods.
